name: MCP Salesforce Server
description: Salesforce's official MCP offering. Primary path is Hosted MCP Servers (GA 2026-04-29, included for Enterprise Edition+ and Developer Edition orgs): Salesforce-managed endpoints exposing org data, flows, Apex actions, and Named Query APIs via OAuth with PKCE, enforcing CRUD/FLS/sharing as the authenticated user. Variants: local DX MCP server (@salesforce/mcp, v0.30.x, Apache-2.0) for dev workflows, and the Data 360 MCP server in developer preview (May 2026).

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Hosted MCP Servers GA
evidence.value: Salesforce hosts and scales the MCP servers on the same managed infrastructure as its REST APIs; structured tool calls replace unstructured API access

crm_operation_reliability.methodology: Assessment of hosted-server operation reliability against the underlying Salesforce API platform

evidence.source: Salesforce Hosted MCP Servers - Standard Servers Reference
evidence.value: Prebuilt standard servers (Agentforce 360 Platform, Tableau Next, Data 360 SQL) plus custom servers exposing flows, Apex actions, and Named Query APIs as defined tool sets

query_and_flow_execution.methodology: Review of tool execution paths for queries, flows, and Apex actions

evidence.source: Salesforce API Request Limits
evidence.value: MCP operations consume org API capacity subject to per-org API request limits by edition

rate_limit_handling.methodology: Analysis of org API limit consumption by MCP tool calls

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Hosted MCP Servers GA
evidence.value: Fully managed infrastructure: no servers to provision, no uptime to manage; Salesforce scales the servers as it does its REST APIs

evidence.source: Salesforce Hosted MCP Servers Help
evidence.value: Tool calls surface standard Salesforce API errors (permission, validation, limits) to the client for recovery

error_recovery.methodology: Error propagation and recovery behavior review

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Hosted MCP Servers GA
evidence.value: OAuth with PKCE controls access; a new MCP-specific OAuth scope separates MCP access from existing REST API scopes, granted via an External Client App

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Hosted MCP Servers GA
evidence.value: Existing org permissions automatically apply - CRUD, field-level security, and sharing rules; every transaction runs as the authenticated user with no anonymous service accounts

evidence.source: Salesforce Hosted MCP Servers - Standard Servers Reference
evidence.value: Standard and custom servers can execute writes (record CRUD, flows, Apex actions) with the user's full permissions; a read-only platform/sobject-reads server is available for lower-risk use such as sandboxes

data_modification_risk.methodology: Write-path risk assessment across standard and custom servers

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Hosted MCP Servers GA
evidence.value: Full audit trails are included; MCP transactions are attributable to the authenticated user in org auditing

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Hosted MCP Servers GA
evidence.value: Secure-by-default: servers are off until an admin explicitly enables them in Setup > API Catalog > MCP Servers and creates an External Client App with appropriate scopes

evidence.source: MCP Data Flow
evidence.value: Contact, lead, account, and opportunity records - CRM PII at enterprise scale - are returned to the MCP client and sent to the LLM provider

crm_pii_exposure.methodology: Data flow analysis of CRM record exposure to the model context

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Hosted MCP Servers GA
evidence.value: Exposure can be narrowed with permission sets, FLS, sharing rules, and by choosing which servers/tools to enable (including a read-only server)

least_privilege_support.methodology: Assessment of mechanisms available to minimize data exposed to agents

evidence.source: Salesforce Hosted MCP Servers Help
evidence.value: MCP endpoints run inside Salesforce-managed infrastructure under existing org trust and compliance boundaries, but responses leave that boundary once delivered to the MCP client

data_residency_and_control.methodology: Data residency and control boundary analysis

evidence.source: LLM Provider Policies
evidence.value: CRM data retrieved via MCP is processed by the connected LLM provider (Claude, ChatGPT, Slack AI, etc.) under that provider's privacy policy

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Hosted MCP Servers GA
evidence.value: Customer PII in CRM records may be subject to GDPR/CCPA; routing it through agent contexts requires customer-side controls beyond what the server enforces

consent_and_regulatory.methodology: Regulatory exposure assessment for CRM data in agent workflows

evidence.source: Salesforce Hosted MCP Servers Documentation
evidence.value: First-party developer guide with a per-server tool reference, setup guides (Setup > API Catalog > MCP Servers), and client connection walkthroughs including Claude

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Connect Claude with Hosted MCP Servers
evidence.value: Dedicated client integration guides published post-GA

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Hosted MCP Servers GA
evidence.value: Servers define exactly which operations are available as structured tool calls, with full audit trails attributable to the authenticated user

evidence.source: npm - @salesforce/mcp
evidence.value: Hosted servers are closed-source managed services, but the DX MCP server (@salesforce/mcp v0.30.14, Apache-2.0, last published 2026-06-23) and the Data 360 MCP server (forcedotcom/d360-mcp-server) are open source

evidence.source: GitHub - forcedotcom/d360-mcp-server
evidence.value: Data 360 MCP server published as an open-source developer preview

open_source_transparency.methodology: Source availability review across the hosted, DX, and Data 360 variants

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Data 360 MCP Server (Developer Preview)
evidence.value: Coverage is explicitly documented per server; the Data 360 preview consolidates ~200 REST operations behind three facade tools (search, payload_examples, execute) to avoid context-window overload

evidence.source: Salesforce Developers Blog - Hosted MCP Servers GA
evidence.value: Setup takes under 30 minutes but requires admin work: enable a server in Setup > API Catalog > MCP Servers, create an External Client App with MCP scopes, then connect the client via OAuth

evidence.source: Salesforce Developer Platform
evidence.value: Performance tracks the underlying Salesforce APIs; complex queries and flow executions add latency versus simple record reads

api_performance.methodology: Performance assessment against underlying API behavior

evidence.source: Salesforce Trust Status
evidence.value: Runs on Salesforce-managed infrastructure with published trust/status transparency and enterprise SLAs

evidence.source: Salesforce Hosted MCP Servers - Standard Servers Reference
evidence.value: Standard servers for Agentforce 360 Platform, Tableau Next, and Data 360 SQL plus custom servers over flows, Apex actions, and Named Query APIs

evidence.source: npm - @salesforce/mcp
evidence.value: Three deployment shapes: hosted servers (GA, Enterprise Edition+ and Developer Edition at no extra cost), local DX MCP server on npm for developer workflows, and the Data 360 MCP server in developer preview (stdio, Java 17+) slated to join the hosted lineup at GA

deployment_flexibility.methodology: Deployment option and edition availability review

strengths: Salesforce-managed hosted endpoints: no infrastructure to run, scales like the REST APIs (GA 2026-04-29)
Org permission model enforced end-to-end: CRUD, field-level security, and sharing rules apply, with every transaction running as the authenticated user
OAuth with PKCE plus a dedicated MCP OAuth scope separates agent access from existing API integrations
Secure by default: servers disabled until explicitly enabled in Setup, with full audit trails
Included at no additional cost for Enterprise Edition+ orgs and Developer Edition
Custom servers expose flows, Apex actions, and Named Query APIs as curated tool sets; read-only sobject-reads server available
Open-source variants: DX MCP server (@salesforce/mcp, Apache-2.0) for dev workflows and Data 360 MCP server in developer preview
limitations: CRM PII at scale (contacts, leads, accounts, opportunities) flows into the LLM provider's context
Write-capable tools (record CRUD, flows, Apex actions) act with the user's full org permissions - over-permissioned users mean over-permissioned agents
Requires Enterprise Edition or above (or Developer Edition); not available on lower editions
Admin-driven setup (Setup enablement + External Client App) adds friction versus one-click consumer MCP servers
MCP tool calls consume org API request limits
Hosted servers are closed source; open-source DX and Data 360 variants cover different scopes (dev tooling, Data 360 APIs) rather than the hosted CRM servers
Data 360 MCP server is developer preview only (stdio, Java 17+) and not yet part of the hosted GA lineup

metadata.license: Proprietary (hosted service); DX MCP server Apache-2.0; Data 360 MCP server open source (developer preview)
supported_platforms: Hosted (Salesforce-managed endpoints, Enterprise Edition+ and Developer Edition)
metadata.api_dependency: Salesforce Platform APIs, Named Query APIs, Flows, Apex; Data 360 APIs (preview)
metadata.authentication: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE via External Client App; dedicated MCP OAuth scope
metadata.status: Active - hosted servers GA; DX server actively released (v0.30.14, 2026-06-23); Data 360 server developer preview

code-generation.notes: DX MCP server is purpose-built for Salesforce development workflows (orgs, metadata, testing)

customer-support.notes: Strong fit: case, contact, and account context with org permissions enforced

content-creation.notes: Useful for CRM-grounded outreach drafting; not a content platform

data-analysis.notes: Data 360 SQL and Tableau Next servers plus Named Queries enable governed analytics

research-assistant.notes: Good for account research within the org; limited outside CRM data

legal-compliance.notes: Strong access controls and audit trails, but CRM PII reaches the LLM provider

healthcare.notes: Health Cloud data routed through agent contexts raises PHI exposure concerns

financial-analysis.notes: Governed access to revenue/opportunity data; FLS can shield sensitive fields

education.notes: Applicable for Education Cloud orgs; standard CRM caveats apply

creative-writing.notes: Not a fit beyond CRM-personalized copy

best_for: Enterprises wanting AI agents on CRM data without building or hosting integration middleware
Admin-governed orgs that need CRUD/FLS/sharing enforcement and audit trails on every agent action
Sales and service teams working from Slack, Claude, or ChatGPT against live Salesforce data
Salesforce developers using the DX MCP server for org and metadata workflows
